body_txt: Being eaten alive. Probably one of our oldest Primal Fears , and quite universal, given that we have long shared this planet with some rather nasty predators. This isn't quite the threat it used to be for those of us who live in parts of the world where humans have made most dangerous land animals extinct or endangered (those in the sea, not so much), but stories of monsters of all kinds ( dragons , aliens , demons , zombies , etc.) remind us that we can become prey again very easily. It's probably the most common fear we have as kids. Sometimes the victim is Swallowed Whole , which will likely entail them learning a "new definition of pain and suffering" courtesy of their devourer's digestive acids (typically the fact they should suffocate long before this can happen will be ignored) but other times, they are savagely ripped apart piece by piece (this is especially common if one is overtaken by multiple creatures out to eat you). Either way is equally horrible. See also Fed to the Beast , where someone is deliberately consigned to this awful fate, and Too Spicy for Yog-Sothoth , for when the eater changes its mind. This is one of the more gruesome ways of pulling a Disney Death ; if someone sees an animal with some clothing in its mouth (especially clothing stained with blood), he isn't likely to wonder about the lack of a body. Super-Trope of Devoured by the Horde , where a character is eaten alive by multiple parties; and Black Comedy Predation , where this, or the implication thereof, is played for dark laughs. Compare and contrast Stewed Alive , which is usually Played for Laughs and involves Black Comedy Cannibalism . Commonly, if not universally, overlaps with Prefers Raw Meat . When done pragmatically, you have Eating the Enemy . Sometimes if you are eaten, you don't have to worry about being digested, because Getting Eaten Is Harmless . Conversely, if you do this, try to be careful you aren’t Outlived by Your Meal . If you were looking for the film Eaten Alive by Tobe Hooper , you'll want this page . A No Recent Examples rule applies to Real Life examples of this trope . Real life examples shouldn't be added until 150 years after the death.
